input_candidates.truth_value: For ferritic steels, cementite shows no effect on elastic modulus; Cr, Mo, Si, Mn increase modulus; C and Ni decrease modulus when phase composition neglected.
input_candidates.prediction_value: For martensitic and ferritic steels (Study I, 53 batches), Cr, Mo, and Si increase the elastic modulus, whereas C and Ni decrease it; Mn and Cu are found to be insignificant in the amounts present; R² = 0.96, standard error 1.2 GPa, maximum deviation 3.6 GPa, average deviation 0.8 GPa.

input_candidates.truth_value: For austenitic steels, N, Cr, NbC, TiC increase modulus; Ni and Mo reduce modulus; M23C6 has no effect.
input_candidates.prediction_value: For ferritic steels (Study II, 35 batches), Si, Mo, and Mn show a strong increasing effect and Cr a weak increasing effect on the elastic modulus; cementite has the highest statistical significance in the regression analysis but shows virtually no effect on the elastic modulus; the weak increasing effect of Cu is considered insignificant due to the large standard error; R² = 0.85, standard error 1.1 GPa, maximum deviation 1.5 GPa, average deviation 0.5 GPa.

input_candidates.truth_value: Regression coefficients can predict elastic modulus for known composition when combined with Thermo-Calc phase calculation for ferritic (study II) and austenitic (study III) cases.
input_candidates.prediction_value: For austenitic steels (24 batches), Ni and Mo decrease the elastic modulus, while N, NbC, TiC, and Cr increase it; Cr23C6 is statistically significant but shows no effect on the elastic modulus; R² = 0.83, standard error 2.9 GPa, maximum deviation 3.1 GPa, average deviation 1.1 GPa.

input_candidates.truth_value: Elastic modulus of pure γ-iron at RT found to be 195 GPa, lower than that of pure α-iron.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Pure γ-iron elastic modulus at room temperature is 195 GPa, which is lower than that of pure α-iron (~211 GPa inferred from the iron regression coefficient).

input_candidates.truth_value: Data dynamically measured with sonic speed, corrected to 20°C; accuracy ±0.3% (~±0.6 GPa); Poisson's ratio determined to ±3%.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Poisson's ratio of untextured pure iron is 0.291; deviations from this value indicate texture; in engineering work Poisson's ratio is often taken as 0.3, but this is not necessarily a good approximation; data with Poisson's ratio between 0.26 and 0.33 were considered untextured and used in the analysis.

input_candidates.truth_value: Texture influence acknowledged but controlled using Poisson's ratio criterion (untextured range 0.26–0.33).
input_candidates.prediction_value: Dynamic measurement of elastic modulus produces the most reproducible results and was the only method used in the collected literature data; the values slightly differ from static measurements.
